sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׁלַם-

(1) to be whole, sound, safe. (Arab. سَلِمَ id. Kindred is the root, שָׁלַו, שָׁלָה.) Job 9:4 “who has set himself against him וַיִּשְׁלַם and continued safe?” Job 22:21.

(2) to be completed, finished, of a building, 1Ki 7:51 Neh 6:15 used of time, Isa 60:20.

(3) denom. from שָׁלֹום to have peace, friendship with any one. Part. שֹׁלְמִי [ Psa 7:5 ] “my friend” i.q. אִישׁ שְׁלֹמִי Psa 41:10. Part. pass. שָׁלוּם peaceable, 2Sa 20:19 see Pual No. 3.

Piel, שִׁלַּם and שִׁלֵּם-

(1) to make secure, to keep safe, Job 8:6.

(2) to complete, to finish (a building) 1Ki 9:25.

(3) to restore, as something purloined, Exo 21:36 something owed, Psa 37:21 2Ki 4:7 to pay, as vows, Psa 50:14 sacrifices, Hos 14:3 and figuratively, to impart comfort, Isa 57:18.

(4) to requite, to recompense, followed by a dat. of pers. Jud 1:7 2Ki 9:26 Psa. 62:13 followed by an acc. of thing, Jer 16:18 32:18 followed by both cases, as שִׁלַּם גְּמוּלֹו לְ (see גְּמוּלּ No. 1 ), also שִׁלַּם לִפְ׳ כְּמַעֲשָׂיו to recompense to any one according to his works, Ps. 62:13 Jer 50:29. There also follows (although rarely) an acc. of the person to whom any thing is paid (Germ. jemanden bezahlen). Psa 31:24 Pro 13:21 וְאֶת־צַדִּיקִים יְשַׁלֶּם־טֹוב “but prosperity rewards (pr. bezahlt) the righteous,” prosperity is their reward, Psa 35:12.

Pual-

(1) pass. of Piel No. 3, to be paid or performed (used of a vow) Psa 65:2.

(2) to be recompensed, Jer 18:20 also to receive the reward (of deeds) Pro 11:31 הֵן צַדִּיק בָּאָרֶץ יְשֻׁלַּם “behold there is a reward for the righteous in the earth, much more for the ungodly and sinner,” Pro 13:13.

(3) to live friendly, i.q. Kal No. 3. Part. מְשֻׁלָּם the friend (of God), i.e. Israel [Christ], Isa 42:19 parall. עֶבֶד יְהֹוָה compare Hiphil No. 2.

Hiphil-

(1) to complete, to execute, Job 23:14 Isa 44:26, 28 Isa 44:28to make an end of a thing, Isa 38:12, 13 Isa 38:13.

(2) to make peace with any one (Arab سَالَمَ id.); followed by אֶת Jos 10:1, Jos 10:4followed by עִם Deu 20:12 1Ki 22:45 but followed by אֶל to submit oneself by a treaty of peace, Jos 11:19 (Compare Arab. سلم Conj. IV., to submit oneself to the dominion of any one; specially to commit one’s affairs to God; followed by الى; whence إِسْلاَمُ obedience or submission to God and to Mahomet; hence true religion, meaning Mahometanism).

(3) causat., to make any one a friend, Pro 16:7.

Hophal, to be a friend to any one; followed by לְ Job 5:23.

Derivatives, שְׁלֹמִית־שָׁלֵם, שַׁלְמֹנִים, שָׁלֹום, שִׁלּוּם, and pr.n. שׁוּלַמִּית, מְשֻׁלָּם, שֻׁלֶּמֶת, מְשִׁלֵּמֹות, מְשֶׁלֶמְיָה.
